title: David Acord

'''David Acord''' is a sound editor who works for [[Skywalker Sound]]. Acord served as Apprentice Sound Editor on [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Attack of the Clones'']], Assistant Sound Editor on [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Revenge of the Sith'']], Supervising Assistant [Sound Editor] on the [[Star Wars home video releases|DVD]] release of the [[original trilogy]], and sound designer (and later re-recording mixer; sound designer/sound editor on film) on ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'', sound designer/re-recording mixer on "''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Out]]''," and sound designer/supervising sound editor on "''[[Star Wars Rebels]]''." Acord was also Supervising Sound Editor and Sound Designer for both [[Star Wars: Episode VII The Force Awakens|''Star Wars: The Force Awakens'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker|''Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker'']], receiving [[Wikipedia:Academy Awards|Oscar]] and [https://BAFTA.org BAFTA] nominations for his work on both films. He was also Supervising Sound Editor and Sound Designer for ''[[Star Wars: Andor]]'', as well as seasons 1 and 2 of ''[[Star Wars: The Mandalorian]]'' (for which he received an Emmy award).

Acord is also a voice actor for many ''Star Wars'' characters. He first provided the voice of the [[GH-7 series medical assistance droid|GH-7 medical droid]] in ''Revenge of the Sith''. Later, Acord voiced [[Rotta the Huttlet]] from ''The Clone Wars''. In 2015, Acord was the voice of a [[Riot control stormtrooper (First Order)|riot control stormtrooper]] designated [[FN-2199]] in ''Star Wars: The Force Awakens'', a minor role which nevertheless proved popular with fans.<ref name="Meet FN-2199">{{SW|url=news/meet-fn-2199-a-k-a-tr-8r-the-stormtrooper-behind-the-meme|text=Meet FN-2199, A.K.A. TR-8R: The Stormtrooper Behind the Meme}}</ref> Acord was also the voice of [[Edrio|Two Tubes]] in both ''[[Rogue One]]'' and ''Andor'' as well as the voice of [[Teedo (Niima Outpost)|Teedo]] from ''The Force Awakens''. He also provided the voice of [[Din Grogu|Grogu]] in ''The Mandalorian''.

{{Bio-stub}}
